button.title: Buttons
button.description: Buttons trigger user actions. Use `usage` to define semantic intent, `variant` to define visual treatment, and `size` to adapt the component to layout density.

items.usage: Set `usage` according to the meaning of the action: primary, secondary, success, info, warning, or danger.
items.flat: Use `variant="flat"` for the default call-to-action treatment.
items.ghost: Use `variant="ghost"` for lower-emphasis actions, dense interfaces, and supporting controls.
items.icon: For icon-only buttons, always provide a readable `title`, `aria-label`, or `tooltipContent` so the component can expose an accessible name.

patternNotes.snippet:
<Button
  usage="primary | secondary | success | info | warning | danger"
  variant="flat | ghost"
  size="sm | md | lg | icon"
  leadingIcon={<Icon />}
  trailingIcon={<Icon />}
  isLoading
  loadingText="Saving..."
  tooltipContent="Add item"
/>

usage.title: Usage
usage.description: Choose the semantic usage that matches the outcome of the action.

variant.title: Variant
variant.description: Variants change presentation without changing behavior or semantic intent.

scale.title: Scale
scale.description: Scale the component according to spacing and density requirements.

states.title: States
states.description: Use the built-in states to keep feedback and interaction rules consistent.

primary.name: Primary
primary.description: Default high-emphasis action for confirm and submit flows.
primary.label: Save changes

secondary.name: Secondary
secondary.description: Neutral action for companion paths and supporting controls.
secondary.label: Export report

success.name: Success
success.description: Positive action for approvals, completion, or confirmation.
success.label: Mark approved

info.name: Info
info.description: Guidance action when you need emphasis without implying risk.
info.label: View details

warning.name: Warning
warning.description: Cautionary action that deserves review before continuing.
warning.label: Review issue

danger.name: Danger
danger.description: Destructive action for irreversible changes and deletion.
danger.label: Revoke access

flat.name: Flat
flat.description: Filled in light mode and softened in dark mode without changing the action meaning.

ghost.name: Ghost
ghost.description: Low-chrome action for dense toolbars, lists, and utility affordances.

dialog.title: Dialog
dialog.description: Dialogs interrupt the current flow to present focused content, decisions, or structured tasks. Use them for content that needs explicit attention without leaving the current page.

items.useCase: Use dialogs for focused tasks, confirmations, or supporting content that should temporarily take priority over the page.
items.structure: Keep the structure predictable: header for title and description, scrollable body for content, and footer for actions.
items.actions: Use the footer for the action hierarchy required by the flow. Some dialogs need one action, some need two, and only some need the full primary/secondary/tertiary set.
items.overflow: When content grows, let only the body scroll. Header and footer should remain fixed within the dialog.

patternNotes.snippet:
Dialog title Short explanation for the dialog.
Body content

structure.title: Structure
structure.description: Use a consistent layout so users can immediately identify the dialog header, body, and actions.

footer.title: Footer actions
footer.description: Reserve the footer for the action hierarchy instead of mixing structural actions into the body.

overflow.title: Overflow
overflow.description: Long content should scroll inside the dialog body while header and footer remain fixed.

basic.title: Basic structure
basic.description: A minimal dialog should provide a clear title, a short description, focused content, and explicit dismissal paths.

description.title: Descriptive header
description.description: Use the description slot for one concise sentence that explains the purpose of the dialog before the body content starts.

footerCard.title: Action combinations
footerCard.description: Dialog footers do not need three actions every time. Use only the actions the task actually requires, and vary the button usage by the intent of the flow.

overflowCard.title: Scrollable body
overflowCard.description: When the dialog body is long, only the content region should scroll so context and actions remain visible.

basic.title: Create workspace
basic.description: Use a short description to explain what the dialog is for before the user reviews the body content.
basic.body: This example shows a common two-action dialog: a dismissive secondary action and a single primary action for completion.

footer.title: Delete project
footer.description: This example shows a destructive footer with a low-emphasis tertiary action, a secondary dismissal path, and a danger primary action.

overflow.title: Migration notes
overflow.description: The body region handles overflow so the title and footer remain visible while the user reads longer content.

paragraphs.one: Dialogs should still feel stable when content becomes long. Header information needs to stay visible so the user never loses context.
paragraphs.two: The body is the only region that should scroll. That keeps the action row anchored and prevents the footer from drifting out of view.
paragraphs.three: Long-form content in a dialog should still be focused. If the content starts to resemble a full page, reconsider whether a dedicated route is a better fit.
paragraphs.four: Descriptions belong near the title, not buried inside the content body. That keeps the purpose of the dialog clear before the user interacts with the details.
paragraphs.five: Footer actions should remain compact and predictable. Use the same action order consistently so the primary decision stays easy to find.
paragraphs.six: This example intentionally adds enough content to force overflow so the scrolling behavior can be evaluated in a realistic documentation scenario.

tooltip.title: Tooltip
tooltip.description: Tooltips provide short contextual hints for controls and inline UI elements. Use the wrapper directly around custom triggers or rely on automatic tooltips for icon-only buttons.

items.clarify: Use tooltips to clarify compact controls, especially icon-only actions that need visible context on hover or focus.
items.reinforce: Tooltips should reinforce an existing control, not carry essential information by themselves.
items.compact: Keep tooltip copy short and neutral. If the content needs instructions or multiple actions, use another pattern such as helper text, a popover, or a dialog.
items.iconButtons: For icon buttons, the button component already resolves tooltip content from `tooltipContent`, `title`, or the accessible label.

patternNotes.snippet:
<Tooltip content="Open details" side="top" align="center">
  <button type="button">Trigger</button>
</Tooltip>

<Button
  size="icon"
  variant="ghost"
  title="Add item"
  leadingIcon={<Plus />}
/>

automatic.title: Automatic icon tooltip
automatic.description: Icon buttons resolve tooltip content automatically from the props already used for accessibility.

placement.title: Placement
placement.description: Choose the placement that keeps the hint readable near surrounding layout constraints.

richContent.title: Rich content
richContent.description: Tooltip content may include small amounts of structure, but it should remain compact and scannable.

triggerGuidance.title: Trigger guidance
triggerGuidance.description: Use tooltips as supporting guidance, not as the primary source of meaning.

top.name: Top
top.description: Default placement for compact controls and icon actions.

right.name: Right
right.description: Useful beside dense left-aligned navigation and stacked lists.

bottom.name: Bottom
bottom.description: Safer when there is no room above the trigger.

left.name: Left
left.description: Works well near right-edge controls and floating actions.

inlineHint.title: Inline hint
inlineHint.description: Wrap custom inline triggers when you need context without changing the surrounding layout.
inlineHint.prefix: Status updates are available in the
inlineHint.tooltip: Opens the activity stream for this workspace.

compactMetadata.title: Compact metadata
compactMetadata.description: A small amount of structured content is fine when the hint needs a title and one line of context.
compactMetadata.tooltipBody: Reference docs are kept separate from the current flow.

avoidLongInstructions.title: Avoid long instructions
avoidLongInstructions.description: If the message starts reading like onboarding copy, use helper text, a popover, or a dialog instead.
